title: Cross-language contract CI and closure
goal: Close the complexity-remediation program with shared Rust/Node IPC fixtures, shipped extension tests in CI, corrected AF-06/operator documentation, and full regression/soak evidence.
acceptance: Use behavioral TDD: first add/activate a Node consumer test against shared fixtures and show CI/package scripts did not previously run the complete shipped extension suite, then wire it into the standard test path.

Canonical versioned JSON fixtures cover daemon status/read-model projections, operator actions, worker and repair wire results, typed/legacy events, terminal summaries, and representative blocked/failed/completed/cancelled runs.

Rust producer/decoder tests and Node extension consumer/painter tests read the same fixture files and fail on semantic/schema drift; fixtures are generated or reviewed through an explicit deterministic process rather than copied independently.

npm test executes all shipped khazad-worker and khazad-monitor tests, including external tests under tests/, and the GitHub Actions workflow installs the supported Node version and runs npm test alongside Rust fmt/test/clippy/check and workflow validation.
README.md and skills/khazad-doom/SKILL.md accurately describe AF-06 promote/run autonomy as active, with daemon-owned authority boundaries, truthful stop conditions, and no stale inactive wording.

A closure note maps ASK-FALLBACK-01 plus CA-01 through CA-09 to the operator-authorized ask_operator fallback and original nine remediation steps, records the confirmed behavioral test seams, lists invariant-bearing protocols extracted, and explicitly records deferred speculative abstractions.

The final evidence includes passing cargo test --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, cargo check --all-targets, npm test, slice validation, roadmap truth check, and the reproducible 1/3/10-worker soak.
No existing test is deleted, ignored, snapshot-updated without semantic review, or narrowed merely to make the closure gate pass.
must_ask_if: Cross-language parity would require duplicating daemon semantic logic in JavaScript.
CI cannot run Node tests without adding privileged services or private credentials.
Documentation truth conflicts with current daemon behavior after CA-01 through CA-08.
Full validation exposes a pre-existing unrelated failure that cannot be fixed within declared areas.
